distinctive_features: appears above cumulus or cumulonimbus clouds
bright iridescent colors due to sunlight diffraction
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: small, typically covering the top of a cumulus cloud
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: forms a cap over the main cloud

domain_specific_visual.formation_context: formed by strong updrafts at lower altitudes

secondary_functions: indicates strong updraft within the cloud

domain_specific_functional.weather_indicator: signals rapid growth and instability in parent cloud

relationships.description: Cap clouds form above cumulus clouds indicating strong updrafts.
Cap clouds can form above cumulonimbus clouds, indicating potential severe weather.
Cap clouds can form above pyrocumulus clouds from erupting volcanoes.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: indicator of weather changes and potential storms
